title: Multipart Upload State, Parts, Checksums, and Cleanup · Runtime Contract: Multipart Concurrency Cancel
summary: A worker starts multipart uploads without durable state, chooses tiny parts, loses ETags, retries concurrent parts blindly, and never aborts abandoned uploads. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the multipart concurrency cancel increment in recoverable multipart-upload state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound part workers and bytes, cancel safely, drain results, preserve completed evidence, and prevent goroutine or buffer leaks.
Persist upload ID, part number, offset, size, checksum, ETag, attempt, and owner so completion uses the exact accepted part list.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Multipart Concurrency Cancel · Theory Multipart Concurrency Cancel
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3363 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate multipart part state, retained ETag evidence, cancellation, and abort cleanup; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
